title: Crypteronia glabra
intro: Crypteronia glabra, also known as the smooth crypteronia or velvetwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Theaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of C. glabra include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, C. glabra is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to relieve symptoms of fever and cough, or used in combination with other herbs to treat digestive problems.
taxonomy: Crypteronia glabra is classified within the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the St. Croix Island family. It belongs to the genus Crypteronia, which comprises a small group of flowering plants. The family Clusiaceae is a part of the order Malpighiales, which is a large group of flowering plants. The order Malpighiales is found within the clade rosid, which is a subgroup of the larger clade of eudicots. This classification places Crypteronia glabra within the larger angiosperm group.
uses: Crypteronia glabra has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is reportedly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, it is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. The plant's leaves and bark are often used to prepare remedies, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine in Southeast Asia. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Crypteronia glabra.

preparations: Crypteronia glabra is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
